title: Tren

{{Quote|The Tren's dedication to order had once again prevailed over their sense of right and wrong.|Airen Cracken, on the Tren's decision to join the Galactic Empire|<ref name="CRO" />}}
The '''Tren''' were a [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] with tough, [[Hair/Legends|hairless]], [[blue]]-[[purple]] skin and simple facial features. Members of the species sought order in their society, a pursuit that sometimes impeded their judgment of right and wrong. The Tren colonized three [[Planet/Legends|worlds]], including [[Nopsin]].

During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the Tren were initially divided over whether to support the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] or the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. As the Tren colonies swore their allegiance to the Empire one after another, a [[Tren resistance|resistance movement]] sprung up to oppose the [[New Order/Legends|New Order]]. When Nopsin voted to join the Empire, the species had become completely integrated into the Empire. Nevertheless, the resistance continued, at least at the individual level. One such Rebel sympathizer was the Tren [[Opit-Wenbruh]], who finished his commission in the [[Army/Legends|military]] and joined the Rebel Alliance. He served as a member of [[Scandium Team]] and [[Brace Team]] as a heavy weapons specialist.

==Biology and appearance==
The Tren were [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]] with stocky builds and thick, [[Blue|bluish]]-[[purple]] skin that was resistant to injury and damage. Each member of the [[Species/Legends|species]] was completely [[Hair/Legends|hairless]] and had five-fingered [[Limb/Legends|hands]]. The species was divided into [[Gender/Legends|males and females]]. The Tren head was largely [[Human/Legends|Human]]-like, with two [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], a [[Nose/Legends|nose]], a [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]], and two [[Ear/Legends|ears]] positioned like those of a Human. Nevertheless, their large jaw and angled forehead, coupled with their pupil-less eyes, made Tren seem to have "simple" faces in the eyes of other species.<ref name="CRO" />

==Society and culture==
A strong desire for order permeated Tren society. That impulse was so compelling that it sometimes superseded the species' sense of morality.<ref name="CRO" />

The Tren had access to [[Realspace/Legends|space]]-travel technology, and they founded three colonies. At least one of those, the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Nopsin]], had a [[Army/Legends|military]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. [[Soldier/Legends|Soldiers]] on Nopsin were forbidden to pursue other occupations until they had completed their tour of duty.<ref name="CRO" />

==History==
The Tren colonized three different planets, the last of which was Nopsin,<ref name="CRO" /> a world in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]<ref name="The Essential Atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> that they reached sometime before the outbreak of the Galactic Civil War. During that [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]-spanning conflict, the Tren found themselves unsure whether to declare their loyalties to the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] or the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]].<ref name="CRO" />

[[Tren resistance|Resistance movements]] sprung up to oppose the [[New Order/Legends|New Order]] and agitate for the Tren to support the Alliance, but a strong desire for order compelled other Tren to back [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] new regime. Eventually, two of the three colonies officially threw their support behind the Empire, criminalizing the pro-Alliance fighters, suppressing the remaining fighters, and wiping out much of their support.<ref name="CRO" />

Only Nopsin remained undecided on the issue. Nopsin eventually held a vote over the planet's allegiance, a vote that the pro-Imperial Tren won in a landslide. The remaining resistance fighters realized that theirs was a lost cause and gave up trying to convince their fellow Tren to join the Rebellion. With that, the Tren colonies became voluntary member governments of the Galactic Empire.<ref name="CRO" />

==Tren in the galaxy==
{{Quote|Opit-Wenbruh, as a young idealist in the Nopsin military, was devastated by the actions of his people…|Airen Cracken|<ref name="CRO" />}}
As Nopsin threw its support behind the Empire, a young soldier in its military named [[Opit-Wenbruh]] found his loyalties divided. As one of the few Tren who valued morality over orderliness, he wanted to finish his tour of duty. However, the many actions of the Empire that he felt were immoral made him sympathize with the Tren resistance and the Rebel Alliance. Opit-Wenbruh waited for his term of service to end and then began a campaign against the Empire in retaliation for the evils he felt that it had perpetrated.<ref name="CRO" />

Opit-Wenbruh eventually joined the Rebel Alliance as a member of the [[Scandium Team]], one of the Rebellion's earliest [[Alliance Special Operations/Legends|Special Operations]] units. The Tren used his military experience by filling the role of a heavy weapons specialist. With the dissolution of Scandium after it suffered heavy losses in various engagements, Opit-Wenbruh served as a weapons [[Technician/Legends|technician]] and designer. He later joined [[Brace Team]], an Alliance group that specialized in using heavy weapons to create a barrier between Alliance and Imperial forces.<ref name="CRO" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Tren were created by authors [[Craig Robert Carey|C. Robert Carey]] and [[Trevor J. Wilson]] for the article "[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives (article)|Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]," published in the [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 11|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 11]] in [[November]] [[1996]]. The Tren character they introduced, Opit-Wenbruh, was illustrated by [[Kathy Burdette]]. Years later, in [[2011]], an update to ''[[Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion]]'' included an entry for the [[Opitha Tren system/Legends|Opitha Tren system]]. Comments made by [[Jason Fry]], one of the authors of ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'', indicate that several new [[Star system/Legends|star systems]] have been introduced as part of the ''Atlas'' project to represent the homes of species, based on context implied by their names. Opitha Tren thus may be intended to represent the home [[Star system/Legends|star system]] of the Tren species.<ref name="Planetnamia">{{JCFCite|url=posts/27848315|board=Literature|thread=The Essential Atlas and Galactic Cartography: Official Discussion|user=[[Jason Fry|jasonfry]]|date=2007-12-11|time=7:59 AM|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20170916153037/http://boards.theforce.net/threads/the-essential-atlas-and-galactic-cartography-official-discussion.21938930/page-43|quote=I wouldn't get your hopes up re alien homeworlds, beyond simple stuff like the Planetnamian species getting a Planetnamia on the map or things Dan and I can account for with a relatively quick reference.|archivefile=File:JasonFry-JCFAtlasHomeworlds.png}}</ref>
